content.text:
---
name: openresearchos-coordinator
description: Run the OpenResearchOS high-bar research loop with OpenClaw tools, local state, reviewer gates, and experiment execution.
---

# OpenResearchOS Coordinator

Use this skill when the user wants to run OpenResearchOS, start a research topic, resume a run, produce a professor demo, or execute the high-bar research-agent plan.

## Working Directory

Use:

```text
~/OpenClaw-Lab/openresearchos
```

## Protocol

1. Start or resume a run with the local runner.
2. Use OpenClaw `web_search`, `web_fetch`, `pdf`, and `browser` to enrich evidence when live research is required.
3. Use `sessions_spawn` for specialist work when the task is broad.
4. Use the local runner through `exec` for deterministic state changes.
5. Require human approval before running experiment code.
6. After experiments, force exactly one next action.
7. Write paper draft only if readiness gates pass.
8. Export OpenClaw trajectory at the end.

## Canonical Commands

```bash
node src/openresearch.mjs start --topic "<topic>"
node src/openresearch.mjs discover --run <run_id>
node src/openresearch.mjs read --run <run_id>
node src/openresearch.mjs map --run <run_id>
node src/openresearch.mjs ideas --run <run_id>
node src/openresearch.mjs approve --run <run_id> --level micro_probe --idea <idea_id>
node src/openresearch.mjs run-micro-probe --run <run_id>
node src/openresearch.mjs write --run <run_id>
node src/openresearch.mjs status --run <run_id>
```

## Demo Shortcut

For local proof:

```bash
node src/openresearch.mjs demo --topic "traceable autonomous research agents that use reviewer simulation and micro-experiments to reduce weak research ideas"
```

## Non-Negotiable Gates

- No one-pass paper.
- No experiment without approval.
- No unsupported claim.
- No execution of untrusted repositories.
- Failed ideas and failed experiments stay in the run.
